name: Trading Vault
description_short: Trading Vault is a structured trading journal built around a Plan → Execute → Review workflow, offering broker sync, performance analytics, and psychology-focused tools for serious traders.
description_long:
Trading Vault is a web-based trading journal designed to help traders improve through a disciplined, three-phase workflow: Plan, Execute, and Review. Rather than simply logging trades after the fact, the platform encourages pre-trade planning with ideal risk-to-reward targets and directional predictions, then guides traders through post-trade reflection to identify what went right or wrong.

The platform offers automatic broker synchronization via MT5, one-click file uploads, and custom CSV imports, making it accessible to traders across brokers and markets. Performance analytics include equity curves, account growth charts, consistency scores, and detailed breakdowns by trading pair, session time, and day of week — helping traders identify their true edge.

Psychology is a first-class feature in Trading Vault, not an afterthought. The journaling interface is designed to capture the mental state and reasoning behind each trade, giving traders the data to recognize emotional patterns that hurt performance over time. This distinguishes it from analytics-only journals that focus purely on numbers.

Trading Vault offers a free-forever tier covering essential journaling features, making it accessible to newer traders, while the Pro plan unlocks advanced analytics, deeper insights, and additional stat widgets. The platform is web-only with no mobile app currently available. Founded in 2019 and headquartered in Panama, Trading Vault has built a loyal following among forex traders in particular, with user reviews consistently praising its intuitive design, MT5 auto-import, and responsive support team.

tiers.name: Pro
features: Best pairs, days, and time window analysis
Ideal R:R tracking and direction prediction

pros: Structured Plan → Execute → Review workflow encourages trading discipline
Free tier available with genuine core journaling features
MT5 automatic broker sync reduces manual data entry
Strong focus on trading psychology alongside performance data
Intuitive, clean design praised consistently by users
cons: No mobile app — web-only limits on-the-go journaling
Small user review base makes reliability hard to assess
Explicit multi-market support (stocks, crypto) not well documented
No public API or third-party integrations beyond MT5
best_for: traders focused on psychology and discipline
